title: 概述
description: 群组方法目录，所有方法均拆分到独立页面说明。
keywords: sdk android v4 chat sdk v4 android channel overview
content: OpenIM Android core 使用群组承载多人聊天能力。本节将 channel 场景映射为 OpenIM 群组；当前 core 没有 open channel 的独立导出方法。 本页是群组方法目录。创建、加入、成员、申请、禁言和群管理方法都拆到了独立页面。 方法索引 | 方法 | 说明 | | | | | CreateGroup /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/create group | 创建一个 OpenIM 群组。适合发起群聊、创建项目群或从业务组织关系生成群。 | | JoinGroup /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/join group | 申请加入指定群组。是否直接入群取决于群设置和服务端审核策略。 | | QuitGroup /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/quit group | 当前用户主动退出指定群组。 | | DismissGroup /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/dismiss group | 解散指定群组，通常要求当前用户具备群主或管理权限。 | | ChangeGroupMute /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/change group mute | 开启或关闭全群禁言。适合群管理者控制群内发言状态。 | | ChangeGroupMemberMute /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/change group member mute | 设置指定群成员禁言时长。适合临时限制单个成员发言。 | | TransferGroupOwner /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/transfer group owner | 将群主转让给指定成员，通常只允许当前群主执行。 | | KickGroupMember /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/kick group member | 将一批成员移出群组，通常要求操作者具备管理权限。 | | SetGroupInfo /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/set group info | 更新群资料，例如群名称、头像、公告或扩展字段。 | | SetGroupMemberInfo /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/set group member info | 更新群成员资料，例如群昵称、角色相关扩展或其他成员字段。 | | GetJoinedGroupList /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/get joined group list | 获取当前用户已加入的全部群组，适合群列表首屏加载或登录后同步。 | | GetJoinedGroupListPage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/get joined group list page | 分页获取当前用户已加入群组，适合群数量较多的账号。 | | GetSpecifiedGroupsInfo /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/get specified groups info | 批量获取指定群组资料，适合会话列表补齐群信息或刷新部分群资料。 | | SearchGroups /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/search groups | 按关键字搜索群组，可按群 ID、群名称等字段搜索。 | | GetGroupMemberOwnerAndAdmin /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/get group member owner and admin | 获取指定群的群主和管理员列表，适合管理页、权限判断和群成员列表顶部展示。 | | GetGroupMemberListByJoinTimeFilter /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/get group member list by join time filter | 按入群时间范围和用户过滤条件获取群成员，适合审计、成员管理和增量查看。 | | GetSpecifiedGroupMembersInfo /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/get specified group members info | 获取指定群内指定用户的群成员资料，适合查看成员详情或刷新少量成员信息。 | | GetGroupMemberList /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/get group member list | 按成员类型筛选并分页获取群成员列表。 | | GetGroupApplicationListAsRecipient /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/get group application list as recipient | 获取当前用户作为群管理者收到的入群申请列表。 | | GetGroupApplicationListAsApplicant /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/get group application list as applicant | 获取当前用户自己提交的入群申请列表。 | | SearchGroupMembers /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/search group members | 搜索群成员，适合按用户 ID 或群昵称查找成员。 | | IsJoinGroup /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/is join group | 判断当前用户是否已经加入指定群组，适合展示入群按钮或发送群消息前校验。 | | GetUsersInGroup /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/get users in group | 查询一批用户是否在指定群组内，适合邀请前过滤已入群用户。 | | InviteUserToGroup /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/invite user to group | 邀请一批用户加入群组。是否直接加入取决于群设置和服务端策略。 | | AcceptGroupApplication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/accept group application | 同意指定用户的入群申请。 | | RefuseGroupApplication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/refuse group application | 拒绝指定用户的入群申请。 | | GetGroupApplicationUnhandledCount /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/channel/methods/get group application unhandled count | 获取未处理入群申请数量，适合管理入口角标和后台同步。 | 事件联动 群组相关变化通过 SetGroupListener 注册的 OnGroupListener 接收，包括群新增/删除、群资料变化、成员新增/删除、成员资料变化、群申请新增/删除/接受/拒绝等。

title: 概述
description: 会话与消息方法目录，所有方法均拆分到独立页面说明。
keywords: sdk android v4 chat sdk v4 android message overview
content: 会话和消息方法来自 open im sdk/conversation msg.go 。创建消息类方法同步返回消息 JSON；发送、查询、删除、已读等方法通过回调返回。 本页是会话与消息方法目录。会话列表、消息创建、发送、历史消息、已读、删除、搜索和输入状态都拆成了独立方法页。 方法索引 | 方法 | 说明 | | | | | GetAllConversationList /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/get all conversation list | 获取当前用户的全部本地会话列表，适合聊天列表全量加载或登录后刷新。 | | GetConversationListSplit /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/get conversation list split | 分页获取会话列表，适合会话数量较多时按页加载。 | | GetOneConversation /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/get one conversation | 按会话类型和目标 ID 获取单个会话。单聊 sourceID 通常是用户 ID，群聊 sourceID 通常是群 ID。 | | GetMultipleConversation /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/get multiple conversation | 按会话 ID 批量获取多个会话，适合局部刷新或从推送跳转后补齐会话。 | | SetConversation /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/set conversation | 设置会话属性，例如置顶、免打扰、扩展字段等当前结构支持的字段。 | | HideConversation /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/hide conversation | 隐藏指定会话，让它从会话列表中消失但不一定删除历史消息。 | | SetConversationDraft /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/set conversation draft | 设置指定会话草稿文本，适合输入框内容离开页面后恢复。 | | GetTotalUnreadMsgCount /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/get total unread msg count | 获取当前用户全部会话的总未读数，适合应用角标和底部 Tab 未读角标。 | | HideAllConversations /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/hide all conversations | 隐藏当前用户的全部会话，适合清理聊天列表展示状态。 | | SearchConversation /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/search conversation | 搜索本地会话，适合聊天列表搜索入口。 | | ChangeInputStates /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/change input states | 更新当前用户在指定会话中的输入状态。 | | GetInputStates /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/get input states | 获取指定用户在会话中的输入状态，适合恢复页面时查询当前状态。 | | GetConversationIDBySessionType /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/get conversation id by session type | 根据目标 ID 和会话类型生成 OpenIM 会话 ID，适合从用户或群跳转到会话前构造会话标识。 | | GetAtAllTag /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/get at all tag | 获取群聊 @ 所有人使用的特殊标记。发送 @ 所有人消息时，应使用该标记而不是业务侧随意拼接字符串。 | | CreateTextMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create text message | 创建普通文本消息。适合纯文本聊天内容，发送前可由业务层先完成长度、空白和敏感词处理。 | | CreateAdvancedTextMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create advanced text message | 创建带实体信息的文本消息。适合文本中需要携带链接、富文本实体或业务范围标记的场景。 | | CreateTextAtMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create text at message | 创建群聊 @ 文本消息。可提醒指定成员，也可以配合引用消息展示上下文。 | | CreateLocationMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create location message | 创建位置消息。适合发送地图位置、门店位置或业务地点。 | | CreateCustomMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create custom message | 创建自定义消息。适合订单卡片、邀请卡片、审批状态等 SDK 没有内置内容类型的业务消息。 | | CreateQuoteMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create quote message | 创建引用消息。用于回复某条历史消息并携带新的文本内容。 | | CreateAdvancedQuoteMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create advanced quote message | 创建带实体信息的引用消息。适合引用回复中仍需要链接、富文本实体或业务标记。 | | CreateCardMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create card message | 创建名片消息。通常用于分享用户资料卡片或联系人卡片。 | | CreateFaceMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create face message | 创建表情消息。适合业务自定义表情包或贴纸消息。 | | CreateForwardMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create forward message | 创建单条转发消息。用于把已有消息转发给另一个用户或群。 | | CreateMergerMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create merger message | 创建合并转发消息。适合转发一组聊天记录，并展示标题和摘要。 | | CreateImageMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create image message | 通过图片路径创建图片消息。SDK 会根据本地图片路径生成消息体。 | | CreateImageMessageFromFullPath /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create image message from full path | 通过完整图片路径创建图片消息。适合调用方持有绝对路径的场景。 | | CreateImageMessageByURL /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create image message by url | 通过已上传图片 URL 创建图片消息。适合资源已由业务侧上传完成的场景。 | | CreateSoundMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create sound message | 通过音频路径创建语音消息。 | | CreateSoundMessageFromFullPath /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create sound message from full path | 通过完整音频路径创建语音消息。适合调用方已持有完整本地路径。 | | CreateSoundMessageByURL /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create sound message by url | 通过已上传音频 URL 信息创建语音消息。 | | CreateVideoMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create video message | 通过视频路径和快照路径创建视频消息。 | | CreateVideoMessageFromFullPath /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create video message from full path | 通过完整视频路径和完整快照路径创建视频消息。 | | CreateVideoMessageByURL /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create video message by url | 通过已上传视频 URL 信息创建视频消息。 | | CreateFileMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create file message | 通过文件路径创建普通文件消息。 | | CreateFileMessageFromFullPath /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create file message from full path | 通过完整文件路径创建普通文件消息。 | | CreateFileMessageByURL /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/create file message by url | 通过已上传文件 URL 信息创建文件消息。 | | SendMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/send message | 发送由 Create 方法创建的消息，并使用 SDK 内置上传和发送链路。单聊与群聊只能选择一个目标字段。 | | SendMessageNotOss /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/send message not oss | 发送不走 SDK OSS 上传链路的消息。适合业务自定义上传、资源 URL 已经准备好或使用 Create ByURL 方法创建的消息。 | | FindMessageList /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/find message list | 按多个会话和消息 ID 条件查找消息，适合定位指定消息集合。 | | GetAdvancedHistoryMessageList /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/get advanced history message list | 获取指定会话的历史消息，适合聊天页向上翻页或首次加载历史记录。 | | GetAdvancedHistoryMessageListReverse /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/get advanced history message list reverse | 按反向方向获取历史消息，适合围绕游标向另一方向加载。 | | RevokeMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/revoke message | 撤回指定消息，通常受时间窗口和权限限制。 | | TypingStatusUpdate /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/typing status update | 发送输入状态提示，适合单聊输入中状态展示。 | | MarkConversationMessageAsRead /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/mark conversation message as read | 将指定会话中的消息标记为已读，适合进入会话页后清理未读。 | | MarkAllConversationMessageAsRead /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/mark all conversation message as read | 将全部会话消息标记为已读，适合一键清除全部未读。 | | MarkMessagesAsReadByMsgID /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/mark messages as read by msg id | 按消息 ID 精确标记已读，适合只确认一部分消息已读的场景。 | | DeleteMessageFromLocalStorage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/delete message from local storage | 仅从本地存储删除指定消息，不表示服务端消息被删除。 | | DeleteMessage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/delete message | 删除指定消息，具体本地和服务端影响以 SDK 实现和服务端策略为准。 | | DeleteAllMsgFromLocalAndSvr /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/delete all msg from local and svr | 删除当前用户本地和服务端全部消息数据，属于高风险清理操作。 | | DeleteAllMsgFromLocal /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/delete all msg from local | 仅删除当前用户本地全部消息数据，适合本地缓存清理。 | | ClearConversationAndDeleteAllMsg /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/clear conversation and delete all msg | 清空指定会话并删除其消息，但保留或删除会话展示由 SDK 行为决定。 | | DeleteConversationAndDeleteAllMsg /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/delete conversation and delete all msg | 删除指定会话及其消息，适合彻底移除某个会话记录。 | | InsertSingleMessageToLocalStorage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/insert single message to local storage | 向本地单聊会话插入一条消息，适合导入历史、补写系统消息或本地展示消息。 | | InsertGroupMessageToLocalStorage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/insert group message to local storage | 向本地群聊会话插入一条消息，适合导入或补写群聊本地消息。 | | SearchLocalMessages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/search local messages | 搜索本地消息，适合聊天记录搜索和按关键字查找历史内容。 | | SetMessageLocalEx /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/methods/set message local ex | 设置消息本地扩展字段，只影响本地展示或业务状态，不应当作服务端消息内容。 | 相关页面 发送消息 /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/message/sending a message/send a message 监听器 title: 概述
description: 推送、日志与文件方法目录，所有方法均拆分到独立页面说明。
keywords: sdk android v4 chat sdk v4 android push-notifications overview
content: 第三方能力来自 open im sdk/third.go 。当前 Android core 只暴露 FCM token、应用角标、日志上传、日志写入和文件上传；HMS、多设备推送模板和推送测试器当前不是 core 的独立导出方法。 本页是推送、日志与文件方法目录。FCM token、角标、日志和文件上传方法都拆成独立页面。 方法索引 | 方法 | 说明 | | | | | UpdateFcmToken /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/push notifications/methods/update fcm token | 更新当前设备的 FCM token，用于服务端向 Android 设备发送离线推送。 | | SetAppBadge /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/push notifications/methods/set app badge | 设置应用角标未读数，适合未读数变化后同步系统角标。 | | UploadLogs /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/push notifications/methods/upload logs | 上传 SDK 日志，适合用户反馈、故障排查或线上问题定位。 | | Logs /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/push notifications/methods/logs | 主动写入一条客户端日志，适合业务层把关键 IM 操作写入 SDK 日志链路。 | | UploadFile /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/push notifications/methods/upload file | 上传文件到 SDK 配置的对象存储链路，适合发送文件、图片、音视频前先完成资源上传。 | 上传回调 | 回调 | 说明 | | | | | UploadLogProgress.OnProgress current int64, size int64 | 日志上传进度。 | | UploadFileCallback.Open size int64 | 文件打开并得到总大小。 | | UploadFileCallback.PartSize partSize int64, num int | 分片大小和分片数量。 | | UploadFileCallback.HashPartProgress index int, size int64, partHash string | 分片哈希进度。 | | UploadFileCallback.HashPartComplete partsHash string, fileHash string | 所有分片哈希完成。 | | UploadFileCallback.UploadID uploadID string | 获取上传 ID。 | | UploadFileCallback.UploadPartComplete index int, partSize int64, partHash string | 单个分片上传完成。 | | UploadFileCallback.UploadComplete fileSize int64, streamSize int64, storageSize int64 | 文件上传完成阶段信息。 | | UploadFileCallback.Complete size int64, url string, typ int | 文件完成并返回 URL。 |

title: 概述
description: 黑名单方法目录，所有方法均拆分到独立页面说明。
keywords: sdk android v4 chat sdk v4 android user blacklist overview
content: 黑名单方法来自 open im sdk/relation.go 中的黑名单部分。它们用于维护当前用户的屏蔽关系；具体屏蔽效果仍以 SDK 和服务端策略为准。 本页是黑名单方法目录。加入、查询和移出黑名单分别有独立方法页。 方法索引 | 方法 | 说明 | | | | | AddBlack /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/blacklist/methods/add black | 将指定用户加入黑名单，适合屏蔽对方消息或限制关系互动。具体业务影响由 SDK 和服务端策略决定。 | | GetBlackList /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/blacklist/methods/get black list | 获取当前用户黑名单列表，适合隐私设置页、拦截列表或关系状态恢复。 | | RemoveBlack /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/blacklist/methods/remove black | 将指定用户移出黑名单，恢复与该用户的普通关系能力。 | 事件联动 黑名单新增和移除通过 SetFriendListener 注册的 OnFriendshipListener 接收。

title: 概述
description: 初始化与登录方法目录，所有方法均拆分到独立页面说明。
keywords: sdk android v4 chat sdk v4 android application overview
content: 初始化与登录方法来自 open im sdk/init login.go 。初始化成功后，SDK 会创建全局 UserForSDK ；后续用户、群组、会话、消息和第三方能力都依赖这个实例。 本页是初始化与登录能力的方法目录。每个方法的签名、参数、返回和使用边界都放在独立页面中。 方法索引 | 方法 | 说明 | | | | | GetSdkVersion /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/application/methods/get sdk version | 获取当前 core SDK 的版本号，适合在启动日志、问题反馈和兼容性判断中记录。 | | InitSDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/application/methods/init sdk | 初始化 SDK 全局实例、日志、长连接管理、本地数据目录和基础资源。应用进程内通常只调用一次。 | | UnInitSDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/application/methods/un init sdk | 释放当前 SDK 全局实例，适合应用完全退出 IM 能力或测试环境重建实例时使用。 | | Login /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/application/methods/login | 使用业务服务端签发的 token 登录指定用户。登录成功后才能可靠调用会话、消息、群组、好友等用户态接口。 | | Logout /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/application/methods/logout | 退出当前登录用户，断开当前用户态会话并清理登录相关状态。它不等同于销毁 SDK 全局实例。 | | SetAppBackgroundStatus /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/application/methods/set app background status | 通知 SDK 应用进入前台或后台，便于 SDK 调整连接、同步和推送相关行为。 | | NetworkStatusChanged /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/application/methods/network status changed | 通知 SDK 网络状态已变化，当前实现会触发长连接关闭并进入后续重连处理。 | | GetLoginStatus /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/application/methods/get login status | 查询 SDK 当前登录状态，用于业务层判断是否可以继续调用用户态 API。 | | GetLoginUserID /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/application/methods/get login user id | 获取当前 SDK 实例记录的登录用户 ID，常用于日志、会话归属和账号切换判断。 |

title: 概述
description: 监听器方法目录，所有方法均拆分到独立页面说明。
keywords: sdk android v4 chat sdk v4 android event-handler overview
content: 监听器方法来自 open im sdk/listener.go ，回调接口来自 open im sdk callback/callback client.go 。 Set Listener 是替换式设置；如果应用需要多处消费事件，应在业务层做分发。 本页是监听器设置方法目录。每个监听器方法都有独立页面说明事件范围和替换式设置行为。 方法索引 | 方法 | 说明 | | | | | SetGroupListener /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/event handler/methods/set group listener | 设置群组事件监听器，用于接收已加入群变化、群资料变化、群成员变化和群申请处理结果。 | | SetConversationListener /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/event handler/methods/set conversation listener | 设置会话事件监听器，用于接收服务端同步进度、新会话、会话变更、总未读数和输入状态变化。 | | SetAdvancedMsgListener /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/event handler/methods/set advanced msg listener | 设置单条消息高级监听器，用于处理新消息、离线消息、已读回执、消息撤回、消息删除和在线消息。 | | SetBatchMsgListener /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/event handler/methods/set batch msg listener | 设置批量消息监听器，适合一次处理多条新消息或多条离线消息，减少高频单条事件处理压力。 | | SetUserListener /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/event handler/methods/set user listener | 设置用户事件监听器，用于接收当前用户资料更新、用户在线状态变化和用户命令增删改事件。 | | SetFriendListener /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/event handler/methods/set friend listener | 设置好友关系监听器，用于接收好友申请、好友新增或删除、好友资料变化以及黑名单变化。 | | SetCustomBusinessListener /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/event handler/methods/set custom business listener | 设置自定义业务消息监听器，用于接收业务自定义通道传入的消息。 | | SetMessageKvInfoListener /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/event handler/methods/set message kv info listener | 设置消息 KV 信息变化监听器，用于接收消息扩展键值信息变化。 |

title: 概述
description: 用户方法目录，所有方法均拆分到独立页面说明。
keywords: sdk android v4 chat sdk v4 android user overview
content: 本页覆盖 open im sdk/user.go 和 online.go 中与用户资料、用户命令、在线状态相关的方法。好友和黑名单已拆分为独立页面。 本页是用户资料、用户命令和在线状态方法目录。每个方法的详细说明见独立页面。 方法索引 | 方法 | 说明 | | | | | GetSelfUserInfo /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/methods/get self user info | 获取当前登录用户自己的资料，适合进入个人资料页、启动后刷新当前用户信息或账号切换后校验本地缓存。 | | SetSelfInfo /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/methods/set self info | 更新当前登录用户资料，例如昵称、头像或扩展字段。只应修改当前账号允许自行修改的字段。 | | GetUsersInfo /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/methods/get users info | 批量获取指定用户公开资料，常用于会话列表、消息发送者资料补齐和好友资料刷新。 | | AddUserCommand /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/methods/add user command | 新增一条用户命令数据，当前源码注释指向用户收藏类数据，可用于按类型保存用户侧轻量命令或标记。 | | DeleteUserCommand /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/methods/delete user command | 删除指定类型和 UUID 的用户命令数据，用于撤销收藏、移除用户侧命令或清理本地同步数据。 | | GetAllUserCommands /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/methods/get all user commands | 获取指定类型下的全部用户命令数据，适合恢复收藏列表或同步用户侧命令状态。 | | SubscribeUsersStatus /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/methods/subscribe users status | 订阅一组用户的在线状态，适合好友列表、会话页和在线状态角标需要持续更新的场景。 | | UnsubscribeUsersStatus /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/methods/unsubscribe users status | 取消订阅指定用户在线状态，适合离开页面、好友列表卸载或减少无用状态推送。 | | GetSubscribeUsersStatus /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/methods/get subscribe users status | 获取当前已订阅用户的在线状态快照，适合页面进入时先拉一次状态，再等待事件更新。 | | GetUserStatus /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/methods/get user status | 获取指定用户在线状态。当前源码实现复用订阅状态接口，因此调用后也可能产生订阅效果。 | 相关页面 好友 /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/friend/overview 黑名单 /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/blacklist/overview

title: 概述
description: 好友方法目录，所有方法均拆分到独立页面说明。
keywords: sdk android v4 chat sdk v4 android user friend overview
content: 好友方法来自 open im sdk/relation.go 中的好友关系部分。它们负责好友资料读取、好友列表分页、好友搜索、申请处理和好友关系更新。 本页是好友关系方法目录。好友资料、好友列表、好友申请和关系维护都拆成了独立方法页。 方法索引 | 方法 | 说明 | | | | | GetSpecifiedFriendsInfo /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/friend/methods/get specified friends info | 批量获取指定好友的关系资料，适合好友详情页、会话资料补齐或局部刷新好友信息。 | | GetFriendList /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/friend/methods/get friend list | 获取当前用户全部好友列表，适合好友通讯录首屏加载或登录后全量同步。 | | GetFriendListPage /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/friend/methods/get friend list page | 分页获取好友列表，适合好友数量较多时按页加载。 | | SearchFriends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/friend/methods/search friends | 搜索好友，适合按用户 ID、昵称、备注等当前搜索结构支持的字段查找好友。 | | CheckFriend /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/friend/methods/check friend | 检查一批用户与当前用户是否为好友关系，适合展示添加好友按钮或发起关系操作前校验。 | | AddFriend /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/friend/methods/add friend | 向指定用户发送好友申请。是否自动通过取决于服务端策略和对方设置。 | | UpdateFriends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/friend/methods/update friends | 更新好友关系资料，例如备注、扩展字段或其他好友关系字段。 | | DeleteFriend /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/friend/methods/delete friend | 删除指定好友关系，适合用户主动解除好友。 | | GetFriendApplicationListAsRecipient /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/friend/methods/get friend application list as recipient | 获取当前用户收到的好友申请列表，适合好友申请入口和审核页。 | | GetFriendApplicationListAsApplicant /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/friend/methods/get friend application list as applicant | 获取当前用户发出的好友申请列表，适合查看申请进度或历史申请。 | | AcceptFriendApplication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/friend/methods/accept friend application | 同意指定好友申请，建立好友关系。 | | RefuseFriendApplication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/friend/methods/refuse friend application | 拒绝指定好友申请。 | | GetFriendApplicationUnhandledCount /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/user/friend/methods/get friend application unhandled count | 获取未处理好友申请数量，适合好友入口角标和后台同步。 | 事件联动 好友申请、新增好友、删除好友和好友资料变化通过 SetFriendListener 注册的 OnFriendshipListener 接收。

title: OpenIM SDK for uni-app
description: OpenIM uni-app SDK entry point for App, H5, and supported mini-app targets.
keywords: sdk uniapp v4 chat sdk v4 uniapp overview
content: Overview Use the uni app SDK when the same OpenIM integration needs to cover App, H5, and supported mini app targets from a uni app codebase. The integration should keep authentication, user identity, message creation, conversation state, and event handling aligned with the rest of the OpenIM SDK family. Platform scope App and H5 builds should use the SDK package and runtime adapter recommended by the OpenIM release you deploy. Mini app targets need additional validation for storage, network, file upload, and websocket behavior. Tokens should still be issued by a trusted backend. Do not generate or hard code user tokens in the client bundle. Core integration path 1. Install the SDK package that matches your OpenIM Server version. 2. Initialize the client with apiAddr , wsAddr , the current userID , and a backend issued token. 3. Register connection and message events before calling login . 4. Send the first text message, then validate message receipt in another signed in client. 5. Add platform specific handling for file messages, push notifications, and background lifecycle. Related SDKs WASM SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview for browser and WebAssembly oriented API examples. Flutter SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/overview when mobile and desktop should be handled through Flutter. React Native SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/react native/overview when the app is built with React Native.

title: Freeze and unfreeze a channel
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for Freeze and unfreeze a channel, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm channel moderating-a-channel freeze-and-unfreeze-a-channel
content: Overview Mute or unmute an OpenIM group to freeze channel wide message sending, or mute a member for a fixed duration. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model Sendbird freeze maps most closely to OpenIM group mute. Member level mute is separate and uses seconds. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Have a valid groupID and a current user with the required group role. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; changeGroupMute: data: ChangeGroupMuteParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; changeGroupMemberMute: data: ChangeGroupMemberMuteParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getGroupMemberList: params: GetGroupMemberParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnGroupInfoChanged CbEvents.OnGroupMemberInfoChanged Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Mute the whole group or one member. await OpenIM.changeGroupMute groupID, isMute: true ; await OpenIM.changeGroupMemberMute groupID, userID, mutedSeconds: 3600, ; Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnGroupInfoChanged , CbEvents.OnGroupMemberInfoChanged . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

title: Overview
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for Overview, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm channel overview-channel
content: Overview Use the channel section to map Sendbird channel concepts to OpenIM conversations and groups: create groups, join or invite members, retrieve channel lists, search groups, and moderate group state. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model A Sendbird group channel usually maps to an OpenIM group plus its conversation. Direct chats are conversations addressed by recvID . OpenIM does not expose a Sendbird open channel object in the WASM SDK. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; createGroup: data: CreateGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; joinGroup: data: JoinGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; inviteUserToGroup: data: AccessToGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getJoinedGroupList: operationID?: string = Promise ; getSpecifiedGroupsInfo: params: string , operationID?: string = Promise ; getAllConversationList: operationID?: string = Promise ; getOneConversation: params: GetOneConversationParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; searchGroups: data: SearchGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; dismissGroup: data: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnNewConversation CbEvents.OnConversationChanged CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupAdded CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupDeleted CbEvents.OnGroupInfoChanged CbEvents.OnGroupDismissed Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Load conversations for the visible channel list and groups for group metadata. const data: conversations = await OpenIM.getAllConversationList ; const data: groups = await OpenIM.getJoinedGroupList ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnConversationChanged, data = mergeConversations data ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnGroupInfoChanged, data = mergeGroups data ; Create a group for member based group chat. Use direct messages for one to one conversations instead of creating a channel first. Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnNewConversation , CbEvents.OnConversationChanged , CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupAdded , CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupDeleted , CbEvents.OnGroupInfoChanged , CbEvents.OnGroupDismissed . Always log operationID with failures. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm refresh and reconnect behavior matches the product requirement. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

title: Send your first message
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for Send your first message, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm getting-started send-first-message
content: Overview Connect a browser client, listen for incoming messages, create a text MessageItem , and send it to a user or group. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model A direct message sets recvID and leaves groupID empty. A group message sets groupID and leaves recvID empty. OpenIM creates the message object first, then sends it. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Have a target conversationID , recvID , or groupID depending on whether the flow is direct or group chat. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; login: params: InitAndLoginConfig, operationID?: string = Promise ; createTextMessage: text: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnConnecting CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess CbEvents.OnConnectFailed CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Create and reuse one SDK instance in the browser session. import CbEvents, getSDK from '@openim/wasm client sdk'; const OpenIM = getSDK coreWasmPath: '/openIM.wasm', sqlWasmPath: '/sql wasm.wasm', ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess, = console.info 'OpenIM connected' ; ; await OpenIM.login userID, token, platformID: 5, apiAddr, wsAddr, ; Create the message object first, then send it. const data: message = await OpenIM.createTextMessage 'hello openim' ; const data: sentMessage = await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID: receiverUserID, groupID: '', message, ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages, data = for const item of data renderIncomingMessage item ; ; For a group message, pass recvID: '' and the target groupID . Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConnecting , CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess , CbEvents.OnConnectFailed , CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | | Messages do not appear in the UI | The listener was registered too late, the wrong recvID or groupID was used, or history has not synced. | Register message events before testing and verify the target IDs with a second signed in user. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

content: Overview Attach application specific data to OpenIM messages with custom messages, advanced text entities, or local extension fields. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model CustomMsgParams is delivered to other clients. localEx is local message state for the current client and should not be treated as shared metadata. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Have a target conversationID , recvID , or groupID depending on whether the flow is direct or group chat. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; createCustomMessage: params: CustomMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; createAdvancedTextMessage: params: AdvancedMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; setMessageLocalEx: params: SetMessageLocalExParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvCustomBusinessMessage CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Choose shared custom payloads or local only state. const data: shared = await OpenIM.createCustomMessage data: JSON.stringify cardType: 'invoice', invoiceID , extension: 'invoice card', description: 'Invoice card', ; await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID: '', groupID, message: shared ; await OpenIM.setMessageLocalEx conversationID, clientMsgID, localEx: JSON.stringify selected: true , ; Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvCustomBusinessMessage , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | | Messages do not appear in the UI | The listener was registered too late, the wrong recvID or groupID was used, or history has not synced. | Register message events before testing and verify the target IDs with a second signed in user. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

title: Generate thumbnails of a file message

content: Overview OpenIM image and video message APIs accept snapshot metadata, but thumbnail generation itself is handled by your app or media pipeline. This guide separates public OpenIM SDK support from product behavior that needs application code. OpenIM model Provide accurate PicBaseInfo or video snapshot fields when creating media messages. Do not expect the JS SDK to synthesize thumbnails for every format. Support status: the WASM SDK covers part of this workflow; the remaining behavior belongs in your application or backend. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; createImageMessageByFile: params: ImageMsgParamsByURL & file: File , operationID?: string = Promise ; createVideoMessageByFile: data: VideoMsgParamsByURL & videoFile: File; snapshotFile: File , operationID?: string, = Promise ; createImageMessageByURL: params: ImageMsgParamsByURL, operationID?: string = Promise ; createVideoMessageByURL: data: VideoMsgParamsByURL, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnProgress CbEvents.UploadComplete Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Generate media metadata before creating the message. const snapshot = await createVideoSnapshot videoFile ; const data: message = await OpenIM.createVideoMessageByFile videoFile, snapshotFile: snapshot.file, videoPath: videoFile.name, duration: snapshot.duration, videoType: videoFile.type, snapshotPath: snapshot.file.name, videoUUID: crypto.randomUUID , videoUrl: '', videoSize: videoFile.size, snapshotUUID: crypto.randomUUID , snapshotSize: snapshot.file.size, snapshotUrl: '', snapshotWidth: snapshot.width, snapshotHeight: snapshot.height, ; Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnProgress , CbEvents.UploadComplete . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Confirm the application owned part of the feature survives refresh, reconnect, and a second device session. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | | A Sendbird method name is missing | The feature is not part of the OpenIM WASM SDK public API. | Use the OpenIM alternative on this page or implement the feature in a trusted backend. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

title: React to a message in a group channel
description: Support status, OpenIM alternatives, and WASM SDK notes for React to a message in a group channel.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm message managing-a-message react-to-a-message-in-a-group-channel
content: Overview OpenIM WASM SDK has no public reaction add, delete, list, or reaction event API. This guide avoids inventing Sendbird compatible methods and lists related OpenIM signatures. OpenIM model Implement reactions as a backend feature and publish reaction changes as custom messages or conversation refresh events. Support status: there is no public WASM SDK API equivalent to the Sendbird feature named by this page. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Do not call non existent Sendbird style methods from the OpenIM client; model the feature with the alternatives below. API signatures No public OpenIM WASM SDK method directly implements this Sendbird feature. Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; createCustomMessage: params: CustomMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; setMessageLocalEx: params: SetMessageLocalExParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvCustomBusinessMessage CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Store reactions in your backend and broadcast updates as custom messages. const reaction = await backend.addReaction conversationID, clientMsgID, emoji ; const data: message = await OpenIM.createCustomMessage data: JSON.stringify type: 'reaction changed', reaction , extension: 'reaction', description: 'Reaction changed', ; await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID: '', groupID, message ; Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvCustomBusinessMessage , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Confirm your UI does not expose a control that implies native SDK support unless the backend alternative is implemented. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | | A Sendbird method name is missing | The feature is not part of the OpenIM WASM SDK public API. | Use the OpenIM alternative on this page or implement the feature in a trusted backend. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

title: Send typing indicators to other members
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for Send typing indicators to other members, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm message managing-a-message send-typing-indicators-to-other-members
content: Overview Send and read OpenIM conversation input state for typing indicators. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model Use changeInputStates for new code. typingStatusUpdate remains in the type definitions but is marked deprecated in favor of input state APIs. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. title: OpenIM SDK for WASM
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for OpenIM SDK for WASM, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm overview
content: Overview Use this page as the entry point for the OpenIM WASM SDK. It explains the browser package, WASM assets, authentication flow, message flow, and where each Sendbird style concept maps in OpenIM. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model OpenIM WASM SDK uses a single SDK instance returned by getSDK . Conversations are addressed by conversationID ; direct messages use recvID ; group messages use groupID ; groups cover the Sendbird group channel style workflow. The browser package is backed by WASM and persists local state in IndexedDB through sql.js. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; login: params: InitAndLoginConfig, operationID?: string = Promise ; logout: operationID?: string = Promise ; createTextMessage: text: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getAllConversationList: operationID?: string = Promise ; getAdvancedHistoryMessageList: params: GetAdvancedHistoryMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; createGroup: data: CreateGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getUsersInfo: data: string , operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnConnecting CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess CbEvents.OnConnectFailed CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages CbEvents.OnConversationChanged CbEvents.OnUserStatusChanged Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Create and reuse one SDK instance in the browser session. import CbEvents, getSDK from '@openim/wasm client sdk'; const OpenIM = getSDK coreWasmPath: '/openIM.wasm', sqlWasmPath: '/sql wasm.wasm', ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess, = console.info 'OpenIM connected' ; ; await OpenIM.login userID, token, platformID: 5, apiAddr, wsAddr, ; Use these SDK areas as the main navigation model for WASM docs: Application: initialize, authenticate, reconnect, and handle tokens. Channel: use groups and conversations for channel like UI. Message: create a message, send it, receive events, load history, and mark read. User: retrieve profiles, presence, friendships, blacklist, and group member state. Local cache: query conversations/messages and merge SDK events into your own store. Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConnecting , CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess , CbEvents.OnConnectFailed , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages , CbEvents.OnConversationChanged , CbEvents.OnUserStatusChanged . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. title: Manage friend requests
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for sending, listing, accepting, and refusing friend applications.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm user managing-friends manage-friend-requests
content: Overview Friend requests are the workflow for creating a friendship when your product requires approval. OpenIM model Sent and received friend applications are represented by FriendApplicationItem . Prerequisites Log in as the current user. Prepare the target toUserID for a new request. API signatures addFriend: data: AddFriendParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getFriendApplicationListAsRecipient: data?: GetFriendApplicationListAsRecipientParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; getFriendApplicationListAsApplicant: data?: GetFriendApplicationListAsApplicationParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; getFriendApplicationUnhandledCount: data: GetSelfUnhandledApplyCountParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; acceptFriendApplication: data: AccessFriendApplicationParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; refuseFriendApplication: data: AccessFriendApplicationParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; Implementation await OpenIM.addFriend toUserID: 'user b', reqMsg: 'Hi' ; const data: received = await OpenIM.getFriendApplicationListAsRecipient handleResults: 0 , offset: 0, count: 20, ; await OpenIM.acceptFriendApplication toUserID: 'user a', handleMsg: 'Approved', ; Returned data and events Watch CbEvents.OnFriendApplicationAdded , CbEvents.OnFriendApplicationAccepted , CbEvents.OnFriendApplicationRejected , and CbEvents.OnFriendAdded . Verify the result Confirm the recipient receives a pending application. Confirm accepting the request creates a friend relationship for both users. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | No pending request | The wrong list direction was queried | Use recipient list for requests sent to the current user. | Next steps Retrieve friend information /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/retrieving users/retrieve friend information

title: Update or delete friends
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for updating friend remarks, pinned state, extension data, and deleting friends.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm user managing-friends update-or-delete-friends
content: Overview Use updateFriends to update relationship fields and deleteFriend to remove a friend relationship. OpenIM model These methods operate on the current user's relationship with target users. Prerequisites Log in as the relationship owner. Prepare the target friend user IDs. API signatures updateFriends: data: UpdateFriendsParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; deleteFriend: data: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; Implementation await OpenIM.updateFriends friendUserIDs: 'user b' , remark: 'Alex', isPinned: true, ex: JSON.stringify source: 'crm' , ; await OpenIM.deleteFriend 'user b' ; Returned data and events Refresh the contact list when CbEvents.OnFriendInfoChanged or CbEvents.OnFriendDeleted fires. Verify the result Confirm remarks and pinned state appear after reload. Confirm deleting a friend removes the relationship from the list. content: Overview Authenticate a WASM user by creating the SDK instance, registering connection listeners, calling login , and handling token lifecycle events. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model Browser clients should receive userID and token from a trusted backend. login starts the session; the connection callbacks tell the UI when the WebSocket is actually ready. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; login: params: InitAndLoginConfig, operationID?: string = Promise ; logout: operationID?: string = Promise ; getLoginStatus: operationID?: string = Promise ; getLoginUserID: operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnConnecting CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess CbEvents.OnConnectFailed CbEvents.OnKickedOffline CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired CbEvents.OnUserTokenInvalid Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Create and reuse one SDK instance in the browser session. import CbEvents, getSDK from '@openim/wasm client sdk'; const OpenIM = getSDK coreWasmPath: '/openIM.wasm', sqlWasmPath: '/sql wasm.wasm', ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess, = console.info 'OpenIM connected' ; ; await OpenIM.login userID, token, platformID: 5, apiAddr, wsAddr, ; Register token and kick offline handlers before calling login . OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired, requestFreshToken ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnUserTokenInvalid, forceSignOut ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnKickedOffline, showSignedInElsewhereDialog ; await OpenIM.login userID, token, platformID: 5, apiAddr, wsAddr ; const data: loginStatus = await OpenIM.getLoginStatus ; Call logout before switching accounts in the same browser session. Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConnecting , CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess , CbEvents.OnConnectFailed , CbEvents.OnKickedOffline , CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired , CbEvents.OnUserTokenInvalid . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. content: Overview Use the application section to initialize the WASM SDK, authenticate a user, watch connection state, handle token lifecycle events, and apply SDK wide session behavior. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model Application state is split between your backend and the browser SDK. The backend creates OpenIM users and tokens. The browser SDK connects to apiAddr and wsAddr , then emits lifecycle events that drive the UI. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConnecting , CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess , CbEvents.OnConnectFailed , CbEvents.OnKickedOffline , CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired , CbEvents.OnUserTokenInvalid . Always log operationID with failures. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. title: Rate limits
description: Server-side implementation guidance and WASM SDK boundaries for Rate limits.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm application understanding-rate-limits rate-limits
content: Overview Rate limiting is a deployment and backend policy concern. The WASM SDK surfaces failures through normal response and event error fields. This guide marks server owned behavior clearly and lists related browser SDK signatures. OpenIM model Do not implement product rate limits only in browser code. Use server policy, gateway limits, and backend checks; then handle OpenIM SDK errors in the UI. Support status: the browser SDK can provide context, but the authoritative action should run on your backend or Platform API layer. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Provide a trusted backend endpoint when the action affects moderation, push, reporting, scheduled jobs, or admin behavior. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; login: params: InitAndLoginConfig, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; createGroup: data: CreateGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; addFriend: data: AddFriendParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnConnectFailed CbEvents.OnSyncServerFailed Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Throttle buttons in the UI, but enforce limits on the backend. try const data: message = await OpenIM.createTextMessage text ; await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID, groupID: '', message ; catch error showRateLimitOrPermissionError error ; For production limits, check the current user, target, and operation on a trusted service before allowing high risk actions. Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConnectFailed , CbEvents.OnSyncServerFailed . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Confirm the backend validates the current user and does not trust IDs or role claims from the browser alone. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | | A Sendbird method name is missing | The feature is not part of the OpenIM WASM SDK public API. | Use the OpenIM alternative on this page or implement the feature in a trusted backend. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

content: Overview Accept or refuse OpenIM group applications and invitations from the recipient side. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model OpenIM models pending group access as group applications. The handler needs groupID , fromUserID , and a handling message. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. title: Enter and exit an open channel
description: Support status, OpenIM alternatives, and WASM SDK notes for Enter and exit an open channel.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm channel joining-and-leaving-a-channel enter-and-exit-an-open-channel
content: Overview OpenIM WASM SDK does not expose a Sendbird open channel object or enter/exit API. This guide avoids inventing Sendbird compatible methods and lists related OpenIM signatures. OpenIM model Use OpenIM groups for member based chat rooms, or build public room discovery and access policy in your backend before calling group APIs. Support status: there is no public WASM SDK API equivalent to the Sendbird feature named by this page. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Do not call non existent Sendbird style methods from the OpenIM client; model the feature with the alternatives below. API signatures No public OpenIM WASM SDK method directly implements this Sendbird feature. Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; createGroup: data: CreateGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; joinGroup: data: JoinGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; quitGroup: data: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; getJoinedGroupList: operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupAdded CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupDeleted CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Do not port Sendbird open channel calls one for one. Model public rooms as groups or own discovery in your backend. const data: groups = await OpenIM.searchGroups keywordList: 'public' , isSearchGroupID: false, isSearchGroupName: true, ; await OpenIM.joinGroup groupID: groups 0 .groupID, reqMsg: 'Join public room', joinSource: 3 ; Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupAdded , CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupDeleted , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Confirm your UI does not expose a control that implies native SDK support unless the backend alternative is implemented. This guide separates public OpenIM SDK support from product behavior that needs application code. OpenIM model OpenIM exposes string extension fields such as GroupItem.ex and ConversationItem.ex , but atomic counters should be owned by a backend service. Support status: the WASM SDK covers part of this workflow; the remaining behavior belongs in your application or backend. content: Overview Filter OpenIM groups by member IDs by combining group lists with member queries or a backend index. This guide separates public OpenIM SDK support from product behavior that needs application code. OpenIM model The WASM SDK can list group members but does not expose a single “channels by exact user set” query. Use server side indexing for large applications. Support status: the WASM SDK covers part of this workflow; the remaining behavior belongs in your application or backend. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Have a valid groupID and a current user with the required group role. OpenIM model Use OpenIM groups for member based chat rooms, or build public room discovery and access policy in your backend before calling group APIs. Support status: there is no public WASM SDK API equivalent to the Sendbird feature named by this page. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Do not call non existent Sendbird style methods from the OpenIM client; model the feature with the alternatives below. API signatures No public OpenIM WASM SDK method directly implements this Sendbird feature. Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; createGroup: data: CreateGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; joinGroup: data: JoinGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; quitGroup: data: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; getJoinedGroupList: operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupAdded CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupDeleted CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Do not port Sendbird open channel calls one for one. Model public rooms as groups or own discovery in your backend. const data: groups = await OpenIM.searchGroups keywordList: 'public' , isSearchGroupID: false, isSearchGroupName: true, ; await OpenIM.joinGroup groupID: groups 0 .groupID, reqMsg: 'Join public room', joinSource: 3 ; Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupAdded , CbEvents.OnJoinedGroupDeleted , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. content: Overview Handle OpenIM SDK failures by reading errCode , errMsg , and operationID from SDK responses and event payloads. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model OpenIM WASM SDK calls generally resolve to WsResponse . Runtime callbacks use WSEvent . Keep operationID in logs so client and server traces can be correlated. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; login: params: InitAndLoginConfig, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getAdvancedHistoryMessageList: params: GetAdvancedHistoryMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnConnectFailed CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired CbEvents.OnUserTokenInvalid CbEvents.OnSyncServerFailed Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Handle failures from both promises and callbacks. try const response = await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID, groupID, message ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnConnectFailed, operationID = reportSdkError operationID ; ; Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConnectFailed , CbEvents.OnUserTokenExpired , CbEvents.OnUserTokenInvalid , CbEvents.OnSyncServerFailed . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users.
