content: Overview Set up OpenIM client SDKs by matching the runtime to the SDK mode. Browser and renderer layers use WASM assets, while packaged Electron application side integrations can use FFI. Products that support both a web client and an Electron app should usually plan for both modes behind one application level IM adapter. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model The browser SDK requires openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package. Serve them from a stable public path and pass that path to getSDK . Electron integrations need an additional runtime decision. Use WASM for web compatible renderer code and use FFI for application side packaged desktop integration. When both modes exist in the same product, decide which side owns login, connection state, message events, and local state before wiring SDK calls into UI components. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConnecting , CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess , CbEvents.OnConnectFailed . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. content: Overview Use OpenIM local storage through public conversation, message history, search, sync, and local delete APIs instead of Sendbird collection classes. This guide separates public OpenIM SDK support from application owned behavior and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model The browser SDK stores local state in IndexedDB through sql.js. Public APIs expose cached data as conversations, message history, and search results; internal database modules are not exported from the package entrypoint. Support status: the WASM SDK covers part of this workflow; the remaining behavior belongs in your application or backend. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. content: Overview Configure SDK logging at login time and use event payloads to capture enough context for browser side diagnostics. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model The WASM SDK exposes logging options through InitAndLoginConfig . The npm type definitions include OnUploadLogsProgress , but there is no public uploadLogs method in the WASM SDK surface, so app level log export should stay in your own diagnostics layer. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnConnectFailed , CbEvents.OnSyncServerFailed , CbEvents.OnUploadLogsProgress . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm message managing-a-message copy-a-message
content: Overview Copy or forward OpenIM messages by creating a new message from an existing MessageItem or merging several messages into one forwarded payload. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model A forwarded message is a new message. A merged message carries a title and summary list for multiple source messages. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. content: Overview Use local insertion only for product controlled system rows, migration, or client side hints that should not be delivered as normal chat messages. OpenIM model insertSingleMessageToLocalStorage and insertGroupMessageToLocalStorage write to local storage. They do not send a message to other users. Prerequisites Create a valid MessageItem . Know whether the row belongs to a single chat or a group chat. API signatures insertSingleMessageToLocalStorage: data: InsertSingleMsgParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; insertGroupMessageToLocalStorage: data: InsertGroupMsgParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; Implementation const data: message = await OpenIM.createTextMessage 'Local note' ; await OpenIM.insertSingleMessageToLocalStorage message, recvID: 'user b', sendID: currentUserID, ; Returned data and events Reload the related conversation from local storage after insertion. Verify the result Confirm no remote receiver gets a new message event. Confirm the local conversation renders the inserted row. This guide avoids inventing Sendbird compatible methods and lists related OpenIM signatures. OpenIM model Implement polls as a backend feature and optionally publish poll state changes as OpenIM custom messages. Support status: there is no public WASM SDK API equivalent to the Sendbird feature named by this page. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Do not call non existent Sendbird style methods from the OpenIM client; model the feature with the alternatives below. API signatures No public OpenIM WASM SDK method directly implements this Sendbird feature. Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. title: Get read status of a message
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for Get read status of a message, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm message managing-read-status-in-a-group-channel get-read-status
content: Overview Mark OpenIM conversations as read and send group message read receipts when the UI displays messages. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model Use conversation level read state for unread counters. Use group read receipt APIs to report and inspect read state for specific group messages. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Have a target conversationID , recvID , or groupID depending on whether the flow is direct or group chat. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; markConversationMessageAsRead: data: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendGroupMessageReadReceipt: params: SendGroupReadReceiptParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getGroupMessageReaderList: params: GetGroupMessageReaderParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvC2CReadReceipt CbEvents.OnRecvGroupReadReceipt CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged CbEvents.OnConversationChanged Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Mark the visible conversation read, and send group read receipts for displayed group messages when needed. await OpenIM.markConversationMessageAsRead conversationID ; await OpenIM.sendGroupMessageReadReceipt conversationID, clientMsgIDList: visibleMessageIDs, ; const data: readers = await OpenIM.getGroupMessageReaderList conversationID, clientMsgID, filter: 0, offset: 0, count: 50, ; Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvC2CReadReceipt , CbEvents.OnRecvGroupReadReceipt , CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged , CbEvents.OnConversationChanged . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. content: Overview Receive OpenIM group messages through event handlers and merge them into the active conversation UI. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model Use OnRecvNewMessages for normal UI updates. Load history separately for pagination and reconnect recovery. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Have a target conversationID , recvID , or groupID depending on whether the flow is direct or group chat. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; getAdvancedHistoryMessageList: params: GetAdvancedHistoryMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; markConversationMessageAsRead: data: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessage CbEvents.OnRecvOfflineNewMessages CbEvents.OnRecvOnlineOnlyMessages CbEvents.OnNewRecvMessageRevoked Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Register message handlers once after creating the SDK instance. OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages, data = mergeMessages data ; ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnNewRecvMessageRevoked, data = markMessageRevoked data ; ; await OpenIM.markConversationMessageAsRead conversationID ; Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessage , CbEvents.OnRecvOfflineNewMessages , CbEvents.OnRecvOnlineOnlyMessages , CbEvents.OnNewRecvMessageRevoked . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | | Messages do not appear in the UI | The listener was registered too late, the wrong recvID or groupID was used, or history has not synced. | Register message events before testing and verify the target IDs with a second signed in user. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

content: Overview OpenIM WASM SDK does not expose a Sendbird open channel object or enter/exit API. This guide avoids inventing Sendbird compatible methods and lists related OpenIM signatures. OpenIM model Use OpenIM groups for member based chat rooms, or build public room discovery and access policy in your backend before calling group APIs. Support status: there is no public WASM SDK API equivalent to the Sendbird feature named by this page. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Do not call non existent Sendbird style methods from the OpenIM client; model the feature with the alternatives below. API signatures No public OpenIM WASM SDK method directly implements this Sendbird feature. content: Overview Retrieve OpenIM message history for a conversation with cursor style pagination. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model History APIs require conversationID , page size, view type, and a starting clientMsgID . Use an empty start ID for the first page when your app has no cursor. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Have a target conversationID , recvID , or groupID depending on whether the flow is direct or group chat. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; getAdvancedHistoryMessageList: params: GetAdvancedHistoryMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getAdvancedHistoryMessageListReverse: params: GetAdvancedHistoryMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getHistoryMessageListReverse: params: GetHistoryMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnSyncServerFinish CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Use the last loaded clientMsgID as the next cursor. const data: firstPage = await OpenIM.getAdvancedHistoryMessageList conversationID, startClientMsgID: '', count: 30, viewType: 0, ; const nextCursor = firstPage.messageList.at 1 ?.clientMsgID ?? ''; const data: nextPage = await OpenIM.getAdvancedHistoryMessageList conversationID, startClientMsgID: nextCursor, count: 30, viewType: 0, ; Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnSyncServerFinish , CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. content: Overview Retrieve total and conversation level unread state directly; use group read receipt APIs for member level read/unread lists. This guide separates public OpenIM SDK support from product behavior that needs application code. OpenIM model OpenIM stores unread count on conversations and exposes global unread totals. Group message reader APIs can list users by read status for a specific message when receipts are enabled. Support status: the WASM SDK covers part of this workflow; the remaining behavior belongs in your application or backend. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Have a target conversationID , recvID , or groupID depending on whether the flow is direct or group chat. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; getTotalUnreadMsgCount: operationID?: string = Promise ; getAllConversationList: operationID?: string = Promise ; getConversationListSplit: data: SplitConversationParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getGroupMessageReaderList: params: GetGroupMessageReaderParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged CbEvents.OnConversationChanged CbEvents.OnRecvGroupReadReceipt Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Use conversation data and unread count callbacks. const data: totalUnread = await OpenIM.getTotalUnreadMsgCount ; const data: conversations = await OpenIM.getAllConversationList ; OpenIM.on CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged, data = setBadgeCount data ; ; For a specific group message, query reader lists with filter 0 for read or 1 for unread. Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. title: Cancel an in-progress file upload
description: OpenIM WASM SDK mapping, API signatures, and implementation notes for Cancel an in-progress file upload.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm message sending-a-message cancel-an-in-progress-file-upload
content: Overview The OpenIM WASM SDK does not expose a public cancel upload method. Model cancellation in your app queue before handing the file to the SDK, and reconcile late SDK progress events. This guide separates public OpenIM SDK support from application owned behavior and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model Once a file message is passed to create MessageByFile and sendMessage , the public SDK surface has progress and completion events but no cancel token. Your UI can cancel queued work before SDK submission or ignore a late result after the user cancels. Support status: the WASM SDK covers part of this workflow; the remaining behavior belongs in your application or backend. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Keep application owned behavior in your app or backend when the SDK does not expose a direct method. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; createFileMessageByFile: data: FileMsgParamsByURL & file: File , operationID?: string = Promise ; createImageMessageByFile: params: ImageMsgParamsByURL & file: File , operationID?: string = Promise ; createVideoMessageByFile: data: VideoMsgParamsByURL & videoFile: File; snapshotFile: File , operationID?: string, = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; fileMapSet: uuid: string, file: File = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnProgress CbEvents.UploadComplete Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Keep a queue item state around file sends and check cancellation before each SDK boundary. const uploadJob = id: crypto.randomUUID , cancelled: false, file ; if uploadJob.cancelled return; const data: message = await OpenIM.createFileMessageByFile file, filePath: file.name, fileName: file.name, uuid: uploadJob.id, sourceUrl: '', fileSize: file.size, fileType: file.type, ; if uploadJob.cancelled return; await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID: '', groupID, message ; If the SDK has already accepted the send, hide or mark the UI item as cancelled locally and let the final success/failure event settle. Returned data and events Treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks when the feature is partial or server owned. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnProgress , CbEvents.UploadComplete . Always log operationID with failures. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm refresh and reconnect behavior matches the product requirement. Confirm the application owned state survives refresh and a second signed in device. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | | A Sendbird method name is missing | The feature is not part of the OpenIM WASM SDK public API. | Use the OpenIM alternative on this page or implement the feature in a trusted backend. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

content: Overview OpenIM WASM SDK does not provide built in message translation or translation engine APIs. This guide marks server owned behavior clearly and lists related browser SDK signatures. OpenIM model Use your translation provider in the backend or application layer. Store translated text as product metadata only when your privacy policy allows it. Support status: the browser SDK can provide context, but the authoritative action should run on your backend or Platform API layer. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Provide a trusted backend endpoint when the action affects moderation, push, reporting, scheduled jobs, or admin behavior. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; createCustomMessage: params: CustomMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; createAdvancedTextMessage: params: AdvancedMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Translate outside the SDK and decide whether translated content is sent or only displayed locally. const translated = await backend.translateMessage text, targetLanguage ; const data: message = await OpenIM.createAdvancedTextMessage text: translated.text, messageEntityList: , ; await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID, groupID, message ; Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Confirm the backend validates the current user and does not trust IDs or role claims from the browser alone. This guide separates public OpenIM SDK support from product behavior that needs application code. OpenIM model Quote messages reference an existing message in the main conversation. They are not equivalent to threaded reply collections. Support status: the WASM SDK covers part of this workflow; the remaining behavior belongs in your application or backend. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Have a target conversationID , recvID , or groupID depending on whether the flow is direct or group chat. Use a backend if replies need a separate thread list. const data: reply = await OpenIM.createAdvancedQuoteMessage text: 'Replying to this', message: parentMessage, ; await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID: '', groupID, message: reply ; Returned data and events When the page describes a partial, server side, or unsupported Sendbird feature, treat the listed OpenIM calls as building blocks. Keep the authoritative product state in your backend when the SDK does not own it. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages . Always log operationID with failures. content: Overview Migrate Sendbird style Web chat code by replacing app/channel/user/message primitives with OpenIM users, conversations, groups, messages, and event callbacks. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model OpenIM does not implement Sendbird collections, open channels, polls, scheduled messages, or reactions as equivalent client SDK objects. Keep those as backend or custom message features during migration. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; login: params: InitAndLoginConfig, operationID?: string = Promise ; getConversationIDBySessionType: data: GetOneConversationParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; createGroup: data: CreateGroupParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendMessage: params: SendMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getAdvancedHistoryMessageList: params: GetAdvancedHistoryMsgParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; searchLocalMessages: data: SearchLocalParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvNewMessages CbEvents.OnConversationChanged CbEvents.OnNewConversation CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Migrate by concept, not by method name. const OpenIM = getSDK coreWasmPath: '/openIM.wasm', sqlWasmPath: '/sql wasm.wasm', ; await OpenIM.login userID, token, platformID: 5, apiAddr, wsAddr ; const data: conversations = await OpenIM.getAllConversationList ; const data: message = await OpenIM.createTextMessage 'Migrated hello' ; await OpenIM.sendMessage recvID, groupID: '', message ; Replace Sendbird open channels, polls, scheduled messages, reactions, and collection APIs with explicit OpenIM group/conversation/message APIs plus backend services where needed. content: Overview Create reports by collecting message, user, or group IDs in the browser and sending the report to your backend. This guide marks server owned behavior clearly and lists related browser SDK signatures. OpenIM model The WASM SDK can retrieve the message, user, group, and conversation context a report form needs. The authoritative report record, abuse workflow, and moderation decision should be handled by a trusted backend or Platform API layer. Support status: the browser SDK can provide context, but the authoritative action should run on your backend or Platform API layer. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

title: Retrieve users in an application
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for retrieving application user public profiles with getUsersInfo.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm user retrieving-users retrieve-a-list-of-users-in-an-application
content: Overview Use getUsersInfo to retrieve public profile data for application users by userID . This can support flows such as searching by user ID before sending a friend request or opening a public profile card. Do not use getUsersInfo to fill a direct conversation title. OpenIM conversation objects already expose showName ; conversation lists and chat headers should prefer the conversation data. API signatures getUsersInfo: data: string , operationID?: string = Promise ; Implementation try const response = await OpenIM.getUsersInfo 'user a', 'user b' ; const users = response.data ?? ; renderUsers users ; catch error console.error 'getUsersInfo failed', error ; throw error; Returned data The response contains PublicUserItem with userID , nickname , faceURL , and ex . Next steps Retrieve a list of friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/retrieving users/retrieve a list of friends Manage friend requests /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/managing friends/manage friend requests

title: Retrieve a list of friends
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for retrieving the current user friend list with getFriendListPage.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm user retrieving-users retrieve-a-list-of-friends
content: Overview Use getFriendListPage to retrieve the current signed in user's friend relationship list in pages. API signatures getFriendListPage: data: OffsetParams & filterBlack?: boolean , operationID?: string, = Promise ; Implementation const data: friends = await OpenIM.getFriendListPage offset: 0, count: 50, filterBlack: true, ; Returned data and events The response contains one page of FriendUserItem . Refresh the first page when friendship events arrive. Next steps Retrieve friend information /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/retrieving users/retrieve friend information Manage friend requests /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/managing friends/manage friend requests

title: Retrieve specified friend information
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for retrieving specified friend relationship information and checking friendship state.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm user retrieving-users retrieve-friend-information
content: Overview Use this page when you need relationship data for specified friends, not the whole friend list. OpenIM model Friend information is scoped to the current logged in user and returns FriendUserItem . Prerequisites Log in before calling relationship APIs. Prepare the target friend user IDs. API signatures getSpecifiedFriendsInfo: data: GetSpecifiedFriendsParams, operationID?: string, = Promise ; checkFriend: data: string , operationID?: string, = Promise ; Implementation Fetch relationship details by specified friend user IDs, and use checkFriend when you only need to know whether a relationship exists. const data: friends = await OpenIM.getSpecifiedFriendsInfo friendUserIDList: 'user a', 'user b' , filterBlack: true, ; const data: checks = await OpenIM.checkFriend 'user a', 'user b' ; Returned data and events getSpecifiedFriendsInfo returns FriendUserItem . checkFriend returns FriendshipInfo . Verify the result Confirm each requested friendUserID exists. Confirm blacklisted users are filtered only when filterBlack is enabled. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | Missing users | The target is not a friend or was filtered | Check the friendship state and blacklist setting. | Next steps Retrieve a list of friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/retrieving users/retrieve a list of friends Update or delete friends /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/user/managing friends/update or delete friends

title: Mark messages as read
description: OpenIM WASM SDK guide for Mark messages as read, with API signatures, usage details, and verification steps.
keywords: sdk wasm v4 chat sdk v4 wasm message marking-messages-as-read-in-a-group-channel mark-messages-as-read
content: Overview Mark OpenIM conversations as read and send group message read receipts when the UI displays messages. This guide uses public OpenIM WASM SDK signatures and assumes the SDK version is compatible with OpenIM Server. OpenIM model Use conversation level read state for unread counters. Use group read receipt APIs to report and inspect read state for specific group messages. Support status: public WASM SDK APIs cover this workflow. Key concepts to keep straight: userID identifies an OpenIM user. conversationID identifies a local conversation row. recvID sends a direct message to one user. groupID sends to or operates on a group conversation. operationID is optional on most methods and is useful for tracing client/server logs. Prerequisites Install an OpenIM WASM SDK version that is compatible with OpenIM Server. Serve openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the browser app public path. Create one SDK instance with getSDK and call login with userID , token, platformID: 5 , apiAddr , and wsAddr . Have a target conversationID , recvID , or groupID depending on whether the flow is direct or group chat. API signatures Relevant public signatures: function getSDK config?: WasmPathConfig : SDK; markConversationMessageAsRead: data: string, operationID?: string = Promise ; sendGroupMessageReadReceipt: params: SendGroupReadReceiptParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; getGroupMessageReaderList: params: GetGroupMessageReaderParams, operationID?: string = Promise ; Relevant events: on event: E, fn: Cbfn : this; off event: E, fn: Cbfn : this | undefined; CbEvents.OnRecvC2CReadReceipt CbEvents.OnRecvGroupReadReceipt CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged CbEvents.OnConversationChanged Response and event payloads use these common shapes: type WsResponse = event: string; errCode: number; errMsg: string; data: T; operationID: string; ; type WSEvent = event: CbEvents; data: T; errCode: number; errMsg: string; operationID: string; ; Implementation Mark the visible conversation read, and send group read receipts for displayed group messages when needed. await OpenIM.markConversationMessageAsRead conversationID ; await OpenIM.sendGroupMessageReadReceipt conversationID, clientMsgIDList: visibleMessageIDs, ; const data: readers = await OpenIM.getGroupMessageReaderList conversationID, clientMsgID, filter: 0, offset: 0, count: 50, ; Returned data and events The SDK response data field contains the updated OpenIM object or operation result. Persist IDs such as conversationID , groupID , and clientMsgID in your application state. Watch these callbacks while testing: CbEvents.OnRecvC2CReadReceipt , CbEvents.OnRecvGroupReadReceipt , CbEvents.OnTotalUnreadMessageCountChanged , CbEvents.OnConversationChanged . Always log operationID with failures. It is the fastest way to match browser behavior with OpenIM server logs. Verify the result Confirm login succeeds and CbEvents.OnConnectSuccess fires before calling the task API. Confirm the OpenIM call resolves and the returned data or follow up SDK event matches the expected state. Confirm the expected SDK event fires in another signed in browser session when the action should be visible to other users. Troubleshooting | Case | Cause | Handling | | | | | | WASM asset requests return 404 | The SDK assets were not copied to the app public path. | Copy openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js from the npm package assets directory. | | The client never connects | apiAddr , wsAddr , token, platform ID, or network routing is wrong. | Register connection events before login and test the API and WebSocket URLs from the same browser. | | The SDK call rejects | The server rejected the request or the current user lacks permission. | Handle the error in catch , log the rejected error with operationID , and verify group role, token, and target IDs. | Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message Send a message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/sending a message/send a message Receive group messages /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/message/receiving messages through event handler/receive messages in a group channel

title: OpenIM SDK for Electron
description: OpenIM Electron SDK entry point for planning WASM and FFI usage across web-compatible UI and packaged desktop apps.
keywords: sdk electron v4 chat sdk v4 electron overview
content: Overview OpenIM Electron SDK can use both WASM and FFI integration modes. They are not always an either or choice: if the same product supports a web client and a packaged Electron app, you typically need to plan for both. Use WASM for browser pages, renderer processes, WebViews, or UI layers that should stay aligned with the web client. Use FFI for the desktop application side, packaged app behavior, native runtime integration, and boundaries that should live outside a browser like page. SDK mode responsibilities | Mode | Applies to | Notes | | | | | | WASM | Web pages, Electron renderer processes, WebViews | Initialize like the web SDK and publish openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js where the page can load them. | | FFI | Electron application side capabilities and native apps | Follow the Electron FFI SDK installation, packaging, and initialization rules. Do not use browser WASM asset paths for FFI. | | WASM + FFI | Products that support both web and packaged Electron | Wrap IM access behind an app level adapter, select the runtime implementation per environment, and avoid competing logins. | Desktop integration notes If the product supports both web and Electron, define an app level IM adapter before wiring SDK calls into UI code. Use WASM in renderer or web compatible layers, and verify packaged builds can load openIM.wasm , sql wasm.wasm , and wasm exec.js . Use FFI for application side integration, and design installation, permission, packaging, and update behavior around the packaged desktop app. If both the main process and renderer process need IM state, define an IPC boundary and decide which side owns login, connection, message events, and local state. Keep tokens out of preload scripts and main process logs. Validate websocket connectivity in packaged builds, not only in local dev mode. Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview Environment specific implementation /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/environment specific implementation Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message

title: OpenIM SDK for Mini Program
description: OpenIM Mini Program SDK entry point for supported mini program runtimes.
keywords: sdk miniprogram v4 chat sdk v4 miniprogram overview
content: Overview Mini Program integrations follow the WASM compatible core API model for user login, conversations, messages, groups, and events, while the runtime adapter must respect each mini program platform's storage, networking, upload, and websocket restrictions. Compatibility with WASM Use the WASM SDK documentation for shared OpenIM concepts and method level behavior. Mini Program pages should document only the runtime differences: initialization entry, asset handling, request domain configuration, file upload limits, and background lifecycle. Runtime notes Configure allowed request and websocket domains before testing login. Verify local storage quota and cleanup behavior for conversation and message cache. Test file messages with the target platform's upload API and file size limits. Keep token issuance on a trusted backend and pass only short lived credentials to the client. Next steps WASM SDK overview /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/overview User authentication /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/application/authenticating a user/authentication Send your first message /docs/chat/sdk/v4/wasm/getting started/send first message

title: OpenIM SDK for React Native
description: OpenIM React Native SDK entry point for mobile applications.
keywords: sdk react-native v4 chat sdk v4 react-native overview
content: Overview Use the React Native SDK when your chat experience is built with React Native and needs mobile runtime behavior for connectivity, local cache, media selection, file upload, and push notification handoff. Integration model The SDK should be initialized after your application has a backend issued token for the current userID . Register connection and message events before login, then keep conversation state in your application store so native lifecycle changes can restore the UI predictably. Mobile considerations Validate login, reconnect, and message receipt after app foreground and background transitions. Test file messages with the media picker and upload permissions required by each platform. Route push notification registration through your trusted backend and platform notification services. Record operationID , errCode , and errMsg when diagnosing mobile only failures. Related SDKs iOS SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/ios/overview Android SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/android/overview Flutter SDK /docs/chat/sdk/v4/flutter/overview
